headline: Nvidia Stock Heads for Record. What Can Give It Another Boost.
summary: Nvidia  stock was gaining early Monday.  The chip maker was headed for a record close and its earnings could provide more momentum.  While Nvidia is still the leading artificial-intelligence chip company, its 15% stock gain this year so far through Friday’s close has lagged far behind semiconductor peers  Intel  and  Advanced Micro Devices  which have gained on excitement about the uses of their central-processing units for AI inference.

headline: Virtue AI Announces Speaker Lineup for CTRL+AI Featuring Leaders From NVIDIA, Capital One, Stanford, UC Berkeley, ServiceNow, Glean, and Splunk
summary: Virtue AI today announced the speaker lineup for Virtue AI Presents: CTRL+AI, its inaugural conference focused on AI Trust, Risk, and Leadership. Taking place June 4 at The Presidio in San Francisco, the event will bring together leading researchers, enterprise security executives, and AI builders tackling the operational realities of deploying autonomous AI systems at scale.

headline: Qualcomm’s 70% Rally May Be Just the Start
summary: While Qualcomm (QCOM) stock has largely been left behind in the AI-driven semiconductor rally over the last few years, the tide appears to have turned for the company, with the stock up by close to 70% over the past month as Qualcomm is positioning for the next phase of AI, one that moves beyond centralized compute and into billions of connected devices. headline: This “UFO” ETF Is Among the Few Real Ways To Invest In The Space Economy
summary: If you have ever tried to buy “space exposure” through an ETF, you have probably noticed the same trick. Most space funds quietly fill the bucket with Boeing (NYSE:BA), Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT), and Honeywell (NASDAQ:HON), then call it a day. You end up with aerospace primes when what you wanted was the companies actually launching rockets and beaming ... This “UFO” ETF Is Among the Few Real Ways To Invest In The Space Economy

headline: Nvidia Partnership With Anthropic And SpaceX Highlights AI Growth And Valuation
summary: Anthropic and SpaceX have launched a major AI infrastructure partnership centered on Nvidia hardware. The Colossus 1 data center is set to deploy more than 220,000 Nvidia GPUs to power Anthropic's Claude models. The rollout is tied to Nvidia's GB300 systems and is described as one of the largest AI compute expansions globally. This collaboration underscores the role of NasdaqGS:NVDA in providing core infrastructure for large scale AI workloads. For you as an investor, this partnership...

headline: Zero Latency Deploys Red Hat AI Factory with NVIDIA for Distributed Neocloud Network
summary: ATLANTA – RED HAT SUMMIT, May 11, 2026--Red Hat, the world's leading provider of open source solutions, today announced that Zero Latency (0.lat), a distributed AI inference network, has adopted Red Hat AI Factory with NVIDIA as the enterprise Kubernetes foundation for its U.S.-wide network. Built on Red Hat AI Factory with NVIDIA, Zero Latency’s neocloud solution aggregates, networks and dispatches AI inference from its decentralized edge datacenters into industrial centers. headline: This Week In AI Chips - NVIDIA Strengthens Board with Strategic Leadership Addition
summary: NVIDIA has announced the addition of Suzanne Nora Johnson to its board of directors, effective July 13, 2026. Her extensive leadership experience across finance, technology, and healthcare is anticipated to provide significant strategic insights for NVIDIA as it continues its advancements in AI and accelerated computing. Johnson's previous roles at Goldman Sachs and current board positions emphasize her expertise and potential contribution to NVIDIA's efforts in shaping the future of AI...
